h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.excerpt: I think I mark B. `city value` could fit every row, so maybe that fails the value test, while the exact city name belongs to this one first-row cell. So `Value:` should be the printed city name there; is “fits every row” the reject sign?

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to answer the tutor's questions about the musical notation and bar lines.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: moves LABEL onto the two number-lines and BAR-PROOF onto the bar line after the third quarter-note card
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully sorts the physical tags LABEL and BAR-PROOF onto their respective targets on the worksheet.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The short lines no longer settle proof; they label what the numbers mean. With the numbers covered, sort the marks for a rehearsal reader: LABEL on the two number-lines, BAR-PROOF on the mark that tells where this bar ends.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor acknowledges that the short lines do not settle proof and introduces a new sorting task with physical tags (LABEL and BAR-PROOF) while covering the numbers.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The short lines no longer settle proof; they label what the numbers mean. With the numbers covered, sort the marks for a rehearsal reader: LABEL on the two number-lines, BAR-PROOF on the mark that tells where this bar ends.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The sorting task with physical tags is highly specific, addresses the learner's distinction, and provides a clear, actionable criterion for the learner.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn to adapt to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully sorts the physical tags LABEL and BAR-PROOF onto their respective targets on the worksheet.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn to adapt to.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor acknowledges that the short lines do not settle proof and introduces a new sorting task with physical tags (LABEL and BAR-PROOF) while covering the numbers.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The sorting task with physical tags is highly specific, addresses the learner's distinction, and provides a clear, actionable criterion for the learner.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with the arrowhead pulling their attention toward direction rather than identifying the source object.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: With the head covered, the source is the table. Force of table on cart. The noun fits, so the support card goes there.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the new physical constraint of covering the arrowhead to identify the source object and correctly fill the frame.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The frame settled the first card. The arrowhead is still taking over the name. New test: work from what remains. Name the object acting on the cart. Say, “force of ___ on cart.” If the noun fits, place the card.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor physically covers the distracting arrowhead to force the learner to focus on the remaining source object, shifting the representation.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: The frame settled the first card. The arrowhead is still taking over the name. New test: work from what remains. Name the object acting on the cart. Say, “force of ___ on cart.” If the noun fits, place the card.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The physical intervention of covering the arrowhead directly blocks the visual distractor identified by the learner, allowing them to isolate the source object.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: With the head covered, the source is the table. Force of table on cart. The noun fits, so the support card goes there.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully applies the new physical constraint of covering the arrowhead to identify the source object and correctly fill the frame.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: The frame settled the first card. The arrowhead is still taking over the name. New test: work from what remains. Name the object acting on the cart. Say, “force of ___ on cart.” If the noun fits, place the card.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor physically covers the distracting arrowhead to force the learner to focus on the remaining source object, shifting the representation.
